comments.cmt: DialogFunc: minimal dialog callback — WM_CLOSE (0x10) ends with 0, WM_INITDIALOG (272) returns 1, WM_COMMAND OK (10000) ends with 1. Used by ShowConfigDialog to show the generic 'configuration' dialog (About box?).
ShowConfigDialog: wraps DialogBoxParamA with DialogFunc and template in TemplateName. Invoked from HandleMenuCommand case 0xB5E (Help->About/Settings menu item).

UpdateMenuState: toggles enable/check state for menu items BC5 (Save), BC4 (SaveAs), CBC, CB2 (TestPlay) based on g_projectLoaded. Also syncs radio-check state for round-time/difficulty/floating-window/reaction-list/preview-zoom/auto-hitjunction toggles. Re-renders all 35 shortcut menu strings by composing '<label>\tCtrl+Shift+Alt+<VK_STR>' for menu IDs 3300..3334.

Main editor window proc. Handles WM_CREATE (1): creates tooltip + status bar + posts WM_COMMAND 0xA (init project). WM_DESTROY (2): cleanup. WM_SIZE (5): resize status bar + active tab. WM_CLOSE (0x10): prompt save-if-dirty then DefWndProc. WM_COMMAND (0x111): routes to HandleMenuCommand; special-cases wParam==10 (init project after open) and wParam==10020 (TCN_SELCHANGE).

Keyboard shortcut router. Called from WinMain message loop for WM_KEYDOWN. Modifier bits OR'd into slot value: 0x10000=Ctrl, 0x20000=Shift, 0x40000=Alt. Matches against 35-entry g_keyboardShortcutTable[i]; dispatches to ActionEditorMain (cells 0-17), tab nav (18-21), sub-tab nav (22-27), or HandleMenuCommand (28-34: New/Open/SaveAs/Save/CompileGame/TestPlay/Quit). VK_TAB (0x09) traps Tab/Shift+Tab to cycle focus through visible editor tab panes.
Shortcut 0 = 'Action*script compilation' -> send WM_MOUSEMOVE (0x204) to dword_7D55E0 (ActionEditor)
Shortcut 1 = 'Action*Script Deletion': if cell>0 delete-cell (20290), else delete-script
Shortcut 2 = 'Copy': if cell>0 cell-copy (20291), else skill-copy (20121)
Shortcut 3 = 'Paste': if cell>0 cell-paste (20292), else skill-paste (20122)
Shortcuts 14..17 = Call Bmp / Pallet / Ani / Skill Window: toggles g_editorTabCheckedFlags[6*i], sends TB_CHECKBUTTON for ID=(i+40987), calls HandleMenuCommand(i+40987)
Shortcuts 18/19 = Tab Back/Forward (main-tab cycle 0..3): posts WM_COMMAND 0x2724 to main window
Shortcut 20/21 mode=1 (Player): sub-tab 0..6 cycle via dword_7D5310 / _531C
Shortcut 20/21 mode=0 (System): sub-tab 0..4 cycle via dword_7D63F0 / _63FC
Shortcut 20/21 mode=2 (Stage): sub-tab 0..1 cycle via dword_7D5DC0 / _5DCC
Shortcut 20/21 mode=3 (Demo): sub-tab 0..1 cycle via dword_7D61B0 / _61BC
Shortcuts 22/24/26 = Character/Stage/Demo Up: posts WM_KEYDOWN VK_UP (0x26) to slot list dword_7D6F30
Shortcuts 23/25/27 = Character/Stage/Demo Down: posts WM_KEYDOWN VK_DOWN (0x28) to slot list dword_7D6F30

jumptable 0040257B default case
jumptable 0040291C default case
jumptable 004029CE default case
Strcpy-append: like AppendPathComponent but without inserting a backslash separator. Used to glue the ' /T' / ' /F' command-line flags onto the KGT2nd_GAME.exe path.

TextInputDialog: modal text-input dialog proc. Reads initial title/prompt from lpString (first 128 bytes = title, next 128 bytes = prompt). Returns entered string in lpString. IDOK=10000, IDCANCEL=10001 or 2. Used by RenameHitJunction/InsertHitJunction/RenameCommonImage/RenameCharacterSlot/etc.
Stops any currently-playing WAV via sndPlaySoundA(NULL, 2) and frees the global pszWavSoundBuffer.
Dispatches a sound-entry (KgtSoundSlotEntry*) by its soundType low nibble: 0=stop/cleanup, 1=WAV via sndPlaySoundA, 2=MIDI via PlayMidiFromSoundEntry, 3=CD-audio via InitializeCDAudio.

BlitPalettedSprite: blits an 8-bit paletted sprite to the 16bpp (RGB555) framebuffer at ppvBits.
Args:
  pixels  = source 8bpp pixel data (byte per pixel, palette indices)
  palette = 256-entry palette. Each entry is 32-bit ARGB-like where the renderer extracts RGB in 5-5-5 bits from positions (>>19 red, >>11 green, >>3 blue) each masked by 0x1F.
           (This layout matches KgtColorBgra stored in the editor's shared-palette arrays: blue << 3, green << 11, red << 19 combined.)
  dstX, dstY

RemapPaletteIndices: de-duplicates the RGBQUAD palette of the BMP-editor's DIB (stru_7D7600 a.k.a. g_bmpEditorBMI).
For each of the imported BMP's colorCount palette entries, it searches the accumulated unique colors and builds a remap table v14[oldIdx] -> newIdx. Duplicates collapse onto the first occurrence. Remaining slots (from the new unique count up to 256) are zeroed out. Finally, the paletted pixel buffer g_bmpEditorPixels is rewritten in-place through v14[], compacting duplicates.

CleanupDIBSection: given a BITMAPINFO + trailing block (at offset +1064 / +1068 / +1072 stores memDC, HBITMAP, old-HGDIOBJ) tears down the DIB section. Called by LoadBmpFileToSpriteDIB before re-creating it.
InitializeRenderContext: zero out the global offscreen DC and the bmp-editor DIB memory-DC at startup.
Tear down any existing DIB section from a previous BMP import.

LoadBmpFileToSpriteDIB: width / height come from BMP BITMAPINFOHEADER offsets +18/+22 (at v8+1 / v8+2 after the 14-byte BITMAPFILEHEADER). Stride calc below pads to 8-byte boundary (1bpp) or 4-byte (4/8/24bpp).
Copy BMP palette (v8+54 onwards) into g_bmpEditorBMI.bmiColors[] as B,G,R + alpha=1.
Create the bmp-editor DIBSection with biBitCount=8. g_bmpEditorPixels will point to the writable 8bpp pixel plane.
Synthesize a 256-entry 3-3-2 palette for the 24bpp->8bpp quantizer.

Registers all editor WndProc classes. Pre-fills 62 x 144-byte class buffers starting at g_windowClassBuffers (0x7D5200) with auto-generated names 'kgt2nd N'. Also registers 8 special classes with static names 'kgt2kCounterInput', 'kgt2kCounterPM5Input', 'kgt2kCounterInput2', 'kgt2kCounterPM5Input2', 'kgt2kWazaNbInput', 'kgt2kWazaNbInput_tab', 'kgt2kHitJunctionInput' (via off_4451F4 / 'kgt2kCommandInput' via lpClassName).

DeactivateEditorTab(classBufAddr): hides an editor tab. If the tab's classIdx is in range 42..45 (BMP editor / palette editor / animation preview / anim tree — the popup ones), clears the visible flag, un-checks the rebar toolbar button (ID = classIdx+40959), and sends WM_CLOSE to the editor pane.

CreateEditorTabWindow(lpClassName, hParent): creates a child editor pane window using the given pre-built class buffer. Switches style + dwExStyle by the class's kgt2nd index (stored at offset +34 of class buffer). Indices 35-38 use WS_CAPTION-like popup style when g_floatingWindowMode is enabled. Sets the newly-created HWND into class_buf[+32].
CreateEditorChildPanel(lpClassName, hParent): simpler variant that creates a WS_CHILD+WS_VISIBLE child. Stores HWND into class_buf[+32] and parent HWND into class_buf[+33].
kgt2nd class index 41 (g_windowClassBuffers[41]) = 0x7D6910. Toolbar rebar wrapper.
kgt2nd class index 31 (g_windowClassBuffers[31]) = 0x7D6370. Main editor tab container.

InsertHitJunction(hWndParent, afterIdx) -- creates a new reaction entry.
Checks free slot count, auto-generates a candidate name ('Attack %4d'), prompts user for final name (must be <=31 chars, unique), then:
 - shifts reactionItems[afterIdx..199] right by one
 - writes the new name into reactionItems[afterIdx].reactionName
 - for every populated character, shifts scripts[488] 4-byte-per-reaction hurt-bind records right
 - for every character's type-23 script-items, calls AdjustByteIndexAfterIns

SwapHitJunctions(a1, hMem, a3) -- swaps reaction slots hMem and a3 across global and per-character data.
For a reorder drag in the reaction list it:
 1. swaps 36 bytes between g_kgtGameSystemData.reactionItems[hMem] and [a3]
 2. for every populated character (g_characterSlots[i]): swaps 4-byte hurt-bind record at scripts[488].scriptName[4*idx+6]..[4*idx+9] = { uint16 scriptId; uint16 effectObjectId }
 3. for every type-23 ([R] Reaction) script-item in each character: swaps single-byte reaction-I

WndProc for the global 200-slot reaction-item list (class byte_7D65B0).
Displays g_kgtGameSystemData.reactionItems[0..199].reactionName in a vertical 16-px-high list with scrollbar.
LMB-drag reorders via SwapHitJunctions (updates per-character scripts[488] hurt-bind packed table AND swaps byte-operands inside every type-23 [R] script item).
RMB opens popup menu: 0xABE1=InsertHitJunction, 0xABE2=RenameHitJunction, 0xABE3=DeleteHitJunction.
Double-click (WM_LBUTTONDBLCLK 0x203) fires 0xABE2 on pop

WndProc for the 'Doing' toggle under the reaction list (class unk_7D6520).
Single checkbox bound to id 100 (aDoing_0='Doing').
When toggled, sets bit 0 of g_kgtGameSystemData.reactionItems[g_currentReactionIdx].isHurtAction (KgtReactionItem.isHurtAction field).
This is the 'Doing' selection referenced in the [R] Reaction codeblock docs: 'If a move is Guarded it won't deal damage unless that hit junction has the Doing selection enabled.'

jumptable 0040C1D5 default case
Block 6: 4-byte hurt-bind count + 6*N bytes (compact hurt-bind entries)
Write commands: enumerate non-empty KgtCommandInput entries (82 bytes each, max 100)
Write reaction->skill map: 200 max entries of 4 bytes each
Write hurt-bind entries: 200 max entries of 6 bytes each
Write fixed blocks: CPU(0x2B66), config(0x74B), story(0x507C), tail(0x11AC)

WndProc for the FA/FD Hit-Attribute Flags widget (class lpClassName).
A custom 160x16 BitBlt'd control that packs a 16-bit parameter word with this layout (from [FA]/[FD] codeblock docs):
  bits  0..3  (4b): M.Number   (hitbox/hurtbox identifier 0..13; doc says 0..19 but widget caps at 13) -- subcase v24=1
  bit   4        : flag bit 0  (Cancel)                        -- v24=2 XOR mask 1<<4
  bit   5        : flag bit 1  (No Detection)                  -- v24=3 XOR mask 1<<5
  bit   6        : f
CommandListWndProc: scrollable list of KgtCommandInput entries. Each row 82 bytes at slot[1]+11110+82*i
CommandEditPanelWndProc: edits KgtCommandInput at index g_currentCommandIdx

WndProc for the Throw Reaction Edit Panel (class unk_7D5A70).
Edits per-character throw-animation parameters for the currently-selected throw reaction (g_currentThrowReactionIdx) of character g_currentEditorSlot.
Fields at &g_characterSlots[slot][1].scripts[508].scriptName[6*idx + 26]:
  +0 (2 bytes): picNo   (KgtThrowActionInfo.picNo, 0..0x1FFF)
  +2 (2 bytes): offsetX (-999..+999)
  +4 (2 bytes): offsetY (-999..+999)
I.e. per-character 6-byte-per-throw-reaction table of 200 entries (1200 bytes

WndProc for the Skill/Command Select popup list (class byte_7D73C0).
Iterates 100 × 82-byte character command entries starting at g_characterSlots[slot][1].scripts[275].scriptName and displays them as a scrolling list.
On click/Enter, stores the selected index (g_skillPickerSelectedIdx a.k.a. word_44E66E) into *g_skillPickerResultPtr (a 2-byte field in the caller's data) and calls MarkSlotModified.
DOES NOT operate on reaction items -- this is the generic skill/command picker used when binding s
CpuListPanelWndProc: scrollable list of KgtCpuEntry. Each row 111 bytes at slot[1]+10+111*i
CpuPropertiesPanelWndProc: edits KgtCpuEntry at index g_currentCpuIdx

ActionEditorMainWndProc @ 0x414940

Top-level parent window for the action (script/skill) editor. Hosts:
  * AnimationTreePanel on the left (256 wide)
  * Action editor tabs (stats/properties) on the right
  * Numeric spinner controls at top: 'script select' (g_selectedScriptIdx)
    and 'step select' (g_selectedCellIdx)
  * Status bar at bottom

Layout in WM_CREATE:
  g_animTreeTabEntry (dword_7D5670) = left pane (0, 72, 256, h-100)
  dword_7D5700 = right pane (256, 18, w-256, h-18)
  dword_7D5

AnimationTreePanelWndProc @ 0x414DB0

The 'tree' view on the LEFT side of the action editor — despite the name it's
actually a flat hierarchical list of all (script, cell) pairs for the current
project slot, one row per cell. Left column = script name, right area = cell
icons (one 16x16 per cell).

State globals:
  g_selectedScriptIdx / g_selectedCellIdx = current selection
  qword_450270 (hi/lo) = scroll offset
  dword_4501C0/C4 = scroll maxima
  g_animTreeLeftButtonDown / g_animTreeRightButton

Per-opcode property editor WndProc. Dispatches to different sub-panels based on the type byte of the currently-selected KgtScriptItem (16-byte slot in scriptItems[] of the active character/stage slot). Used for [I]/[M]/[FA]/[FD]/[R]/[O]/[S]/[V]/[COLOR]/[AI] etc.
Switch jump on opcode-type (low byte of KgtScriptItem at cellIdx). 42 cases map to the per-opcode sub-editor; default case 13/28/29/33/34/38-40 = unused opcode codes
case 0 (START) - Stage/position-marker editor, and used for empty cells | jumptable 004196AC case 0
case 41 (0x29) - triggers a layout rebuild to opcode 1 (MOVE) — used when switching between compatible-layout opcodes | jumptable 004196AC case 41

case 1 (MOVE) [M] - accelX/moveX/moveY/accelY/flags. Dialog IDs 0x3F2/0x3F3 = Replace/Add radio. Control descriptors 0x4502F0 (accelX @item+1), 0x450310 (moveX @item+3), 0x450330 (moveY @item+5), 0x450350 (accelY @item+7). Stop checkboxes 0x3F4..0x3F7 via item+9 &1/&2/&4/&8/&0x10 | jumptable 004196AC case 1

case 12 (PIC) [I] - ShowPic: item+1 keepTime, item+3 idxAndFlip (& 0x1FFF pic idx, & 0x4000 flipX, & 0x8000 flipY), item+5 offsetX, item+7 offsetY, item+9 fixDir. Control IDs: 0x909 Turn X, 0x90A Turn Y, 0x90B Ignore Direction | jumptable 004196AC case 12

case 10 (JUMP) [SG] GoTo Skill - JumpCmd: item+1 jumpId (word), item+3 jumpPos (byte). Control descriptor 0x4502D0 (skill-name label), 0x4502EC (scriptId word), 0x45030C (pos byte) | jumptable 004196AC case 10
case 11 (CALL) [SC] Jump+Return - same layout as JUMP | jumptable 004196AC case 11

case 2 [DS] Detect Skill Divergence - combobox 0x64A = divergence type (item+1), two numeric controls for scriptId (item+2..3) + pos (item+4). 7 types: Not/Landing/Attack Hits/Defending/Hit Wall/In Offset/While Throw Do | jumptable 004196AC case 2
case 3 (SOUND) [S] - PlaySoundCmd: item+1 unknown, item+2..3 soundIdx. Populates combobox 0x450554 with sound file names from g_projectSlot.soundHeaders[], Loop checkbox | jumptable 004196AC case 3

case 4 (OBJECT) [O] - ObjectCmd: item+1 flags, item+2..3 targetScriptId, item+4 targetPos, item+5..6 targetScriptIdIfExists, item+7 targetPosIfExists, item+8..9 posX, item+10..11 posY, item+12 manageNo, item+13 layer. Checkboxes: Parent (flags&0x20), UnCond (flags&4), Shadow (flags&8), XY (flags&0x40) | jumptable 004196AC case 4

case 7 [RC] Change Shape's Condition (Common Image) - 3 checkboxes Turn X/Turn Y/Same (IDs 0x85C/0x85D/0x85E from item+1 bits 2/3/4), 2 comboboxes: 0x848 throw-reaction/hit-junction picker (item+2..3), 0x852 player/character picker (sel = g_currentCharacterIdx). Position X item+4..5, Y item+6..7. Radio 0x83F/0x840 = In/Out Depth (item+1 & 1) | jumptable 004196AC case 7

case 20 [RP] Change Skill (Partner: Script Mod) - 1 checkbox Turn X (ID 0xB18 from item+1 bit 2), combobox 0xB40 populated from g_kgtGameSystemData.reactionItems[] (HitJunction), position X/Y item+4..7, Radio 0xAFB/0xAFC = In/Out Depth (item+1 & 1), final sel = item[0] (first byte) via dword_45040C + item+2 | jumptable 004196AC case 20
case 5 (END) [E] - End opcode: no fields. Jumps to common empty handler at 0x41B77D, ebx=5 | jumptable 004196AC case 5
case 6 - unused opcode, empty editor | jumptable 004196AC case 6
Common empty-editor tail. Destroys all child controls (WM_COMMAND wParam=2) and caches opcode code in g_codeblockEditor_activeOpcodeLayout | jumptable 004196AC case 8
case 9 (LOOP) [SF] - LoopCmd: item+1 loopCount, item+2..3 targetScriptId, item+4 targetPos | jumptable 004196AC case 9

case 14 [EB] Background Effect - combobox 0x96A = palette-flash type (Unused/Smooth/Chika/Random, item+1), color descriptors 0x4502D0..0x4503D0, Shake-BG descriptors 0x450590/0x450594 | jumptable 004196AC case 14
case 15 [GS] Special Gauge Check - combobox 0x9CE (Little/Alot, item+1), Guage value (item+2..3), Add to advance (item+4..5), skill descriptor for 'If Failed' jump | jumptable 004196AC case 15
case 16 [GL] Life Gauge Check - Little/Alot + Life value + 'If Failed' skill jump | jumptable 004196AC case 16
case 17 - Command-input divergence [COM]? combobox at item+2..3 + item+4..5 + item+6..7 (5 command inputs) + Radio 0xA97/0xA98 at item+5 | jumptable 004196AC case 17
case 18 - Divergence variant: 3 numeric fields (item+2..3, item+4, item+5, item+7) + Radio 0xFAB/0xFAC at item+1&1 + checkbox at item+1&8 = likely [DB] Basic Divergence | jumptable 004196AC case 18
case 19 [RS?] - 'Script for special character' editor with single numeric at item+2..3 + skill selector at item+4 | jumptable 004196AC case 19
case 21 [GC] Change Gauge - 4 numerics: Yours Life (item+2..3), Yours Special (item+4..5), His Life (item+6..7), His Special (item+8..9) | jumptable 004196AC case 21
case 22 - Combobox + 2 numerics + radio 0x113B/0x113C at item+1&1 + combobox 0x113A from item+7 | jumptable 004196AC case 22
case 23 [C] Cancel Condition - 5 numeric slots (items+1/3/5/7/9) as 5 level thresholds. Control descriptors 0x450550..0x450560 | jumptable 004196AC case 23

case 24 [FA] Attack Frame - hitbox: item+1..2 topLeft, item+3..4 topRight, item+5..6 X-radius, item+7..8 Y-radius, item+9 M.Number, item+0xA flags (Cancel/NoDetect/ContHits/NoSkyDecision/GuardFail/WhileGuard/WhileRecv/Shave - 8 checkboxes), item+0xB Power, item+0xC Ratio. Sets g_codeblockEditor_activeCodeblockKind = 0x14 (hitbox edit mode) | jumptable 004196AC case 24

case 25 [FD] Defense Frame - hurtbox: item+1..2 topLeft, item+3..4 topRight, item+5..6 X-radius, item+7..8 Y-radius, item+9 M.Number, item+0xA flags (Striking/Doing/Throwing 3 checkboxes), item+0xB Ratio, item+0xC combobox at 0x1266. Sets g_codeblockEditor_activeCodeblockKind = 0x15 (hurtbox edit mode) | jumptable 004196AC case 25
case 26 [PS] Player Stop (Time Stop) - Your Time (item+1) + His Time (item+3) | jumptable 004196AC case 26

case 27 [DB] Basic Divergence - combobox 0x132E = 8 setting options (item+1), 3 numerics (item+2,+3,+4,+5), Skill selection. Sets g_codeblockEditor_activeCodeblockKind = 0x0E | jumptable 004196AC case 27

case 30 [V] Variable - VariableCmd: item+1 opFlags (bits 0-1 operation None/Assign/Add, bits 2-3 condition None/Eq/Gt/Lt, bit 7 CompareWithVar). Multiple conditionally-enabled numerics for operationValue/compareValue | jumptable 004196AC case 30

case 31 [V] Variable (alt) - VariableCmd full editor: item+1..2 targetScriptId, item+3 targetPos, item+4 targetVariable (combobox 0x450554 via byte_4547E1 lookup table for var encoding Task/Char/System/Data), item+5 opFlags, item+6 compareVariable, item+7..8 operationValue, item+9..10 compareValue | jumptable 004196AC case 31
case 32 [R] Random (RandomCmd) - item+1..2 randomMaxVal, item+3..4 moreThanVal, item+5 unknownGap, item+6..7 targetScriptId, item+8 targetPos | jumptable 004196AC case 32

case 35 [COLOR] Color Modification - ColorSetCmd: item+1 colorBlendType (combobox 0x450550, 5 options Normal/50%Alpha/AddBlend/MinusBlend/AlphaBlend), item+2 red, item+3 green, item+4 blue, item+5 alpha (alpha-enabled only if blendType=4) | jumptable 004196AC case 35
case 36 - 4-numeric editor with combobox/depth flag item+5&0x20 | jumptable 004196AC case 36

case 37 [AI] After Image - AfterimageCmd: item+3..4 numFrames/time, item+5 colorBlendType (combobox 0x45055C, 5 options Normal/50%/Add/Minus/Alpha), item+6 afterimageColorType (combobox 0x450558, 5 options Unused/Fixing/SmoothFade/Chika/Random), item+7 red, item+8 green, item+9 blue, item+0xA alpha | jumptable 004196AC case 37

WndProc for the Hit-Junction Editor tab container (class unk_7D5C20).
Hosts column headers 'Hit Junction' (x=5), 'Alloment' (x=205), 'Spark' (x=405) and embeds HitJunctionListPanelWndProc (class byte_7D5B90) as the editable body.

WndProc for the per-character Hit-Junction Binding Panel (class byte_7D5B90).
Shows a 3-column table of all 200 reactions for character g_currentEditorSlot:
  col 1 (x=5..199):    reactionName (from g_kgtGameSystemData.reactionItems[i].reactionName)
  col 2 (x=205..399):  name of the hurt-animation skill (lookup into scripts[] via 1-byte script index at scripts[488].scriptName[4*i+6])
  col 3 (x=405..):     name of the hit-spark/effect skill (scripts[488].scriptName[4*i+8])
Each reaction i owns
StoryModeSettingsPanelWndProc: gates whether story mode is enabled for this character (via playerSelectableInfos[slotIdx] & 1)
StoryEventListPanelWndProc: scrollable list of KgtStoryEntry (206 bytes each). 100 max.

jumptable 00422E7A default case, cases 8-19,21-29,31-39
jumptable 00424AC1 default case, cases 2038,2039,2042-2049,2051-2059
jumptable 00424C90 default case, cases 3011-3019,3021-3029

Import-bg helper — loads .stage file into stage slot stageIdx.

Backs up lpString (256 bytes) → copies the caller-supplied KgtNameInfo into g_kgtGameSystemData.stageNames[stageIdx] → calls LoadStageFile(stageIdx) (which uses the stageNames[] entry to build the path). On success copies the backed-up name back into both g_stageSlots[stageIdx]->header.name and g_kgtGameSystemData.stageNames[stageIdx].

Import-bg dialog — ShowOpenFileDialog('*.stage'), parses base name from path (strip extension + directory), uniquifies via TextInputDialog if duplicate/invalid, opens slot a2 by memmoving stages [a2..48] down one slot (g_stageSlots[], g_stageSlotFlags[], stageNames[]) to make room, zero-inits the new slot, then calls ImportBgFromNamedFile to actually load the .stage data.
Import-demo helper — identical shape to ImportBgFromNamedFile but for demos: copies KgtNameInfo into g_kgtGameSystemData.demoNames[demoIdx], calls LoadDemoFile(demoIdx), restores the backed-up name.

Import-demo dialog — ShowOpenFileDialog('*.demo'), strip/validate base name, uniquify via TextInputDialog, memmove [a2..98] down one demo slot, zero-init, ImportDemoFromNamedFile to load. After load: fixup demoConfig.titleDemoId[8] and stage-slot scripts via AdjustWordIndexAfterInsert.

jumptable 0042BF77 default case, cases 3,4,6
jumptable 0042C607 default case, cases 278-511,515

PaletteRemapDlgProc: loops over the 8192-entry pictureHeaders array (stride 20 = sizeof KgtPictureSlotEntry), for each sprite: decompress (if compressed via DecompressSpriteData), remap every pixel through g_paletteRemapTable[256], re-store the result. Private-palette sprites skip the first 1024 bytes (the palette prefix). Progressbar (dword_451674) is ticked per-sprite.

ShowPaletteRemapDialog: launches the 'Pal Remap' progress dialog (id A101, handler PaletteRemapDlgProc). The dialog's WM_INITDIALOG (272) sets up a progressbar; WM_CLOSE-ish (15) iterates g_projectSlot.pictureHeaders[8192], and for every picture rewrites its 8bpp pixel data by applying g_paletteRemapTable[] in place, handling both compressed (has non-zero .size field) and uncompressed sprites plus private-palette offset of 1024 bytes.

SortPaletteDarkToLight: compute brightness key = (r>>3)+(g>>3)+(b>>3) for each of the 256 colors in the currently-selected shared palette; active (alpha!=0) slots get +1, inactive zero-RGB slots get -1 so they sort last. Selection sort: when swapping, mirror the swap across ALL 8 shared palettes (so all 8 remain aligned index-wise). Also fill g_paletteRemapTable[oldIdx] = newIdx and call ShowPaletteRemapDialog which rewrites every sprite's pixel data.

FindAndSwapPaletteColor(r5, g5, b5, &cursor, backMap): scan from cursor upward; find first active palette entry matching the 5-bit (r,g,b) quantized RGB in the currently-selected shared palette. If found, swap it with position *cursor across ALL 8 shared palettes (loop j=0..2112 step 264 covers the 8 palettes offset by 1056/4 dwords each), update backMap (sort-stable index), increment *cursor.

SortPaletteByChannel: sort colors by *dominant channel* into a specific order. For each 5-bit R value (31..0) fill positions where max(R,G,B)==R; then by G-dominance, then B-dominance. Calls FindAndSwapPaletteColor to do the in-place reordering of all 8 shared palettes simultaneously.
ColorCountInputDlgProc: 'Enter new color count (1-N)' dialog for ReducePaletteColors. Writes result to g_paletteReduceTargetCount.

ReducePaletteColors: iteratively merges the two most-similar active palette entries until the active-count drops to g_paletteReduceTargetCount (entered via ColorCountInputDlg). Similarity metric = sum-of-3x quantized channel sums / 10 + squared-distance in 8-bit RGB space. Found pair: overwrite slot v6 with averaged-and-quantized color (&0xF8 per channel), zero out slot v22 across all 8 shared palettes, update g_paletteRemapTable[v22] = v6 (and any previously-remapped entries). At the end calls

PaletteEditorWndProc: the interactive palette-editor window.
Visualization: paints the 16x16 grid of 256 colors from g_projectSlot.sharedPalettes[g_selectedPaletteIdx] (byte offset 1252624 within the slot). Alpha byte (index+3) indicates the slot is 'active' (1) or empty (0); empty slots show a white dot.
Mouse: WM_LBUTTONDBLCLK (Msg 515) calls ChooseColor on the clicked palette entry; if the new color duplicates an existing one, prompts to reuse the duplicate.
WM_MOUSEMOVE updates (g_paletteEdi

SkillListPanelWndProc @ 0x4314A0

The skill-list panel that shows all named scripts (skills) for the current character/slot.
Each row is 16 px tall. Items enumerated by walking g_activeSlotPtr->scripts[1024],
filtering to entries with non-empty scriptName.

State globals:
  g_skillPickerSelectedIdx (WORD) = currently selected skill row
  qword_451728 = scroll offset (hi=y-scroll, lo=x-scroll)
  dword_451730/4 = scroll maxima (width/height in cells)
  g_skillListPopupHostHwnd = parent window to n

SkillCellGridPanelWndProc @ 0x431CC0

Horizontal codeblock-cell timeline for the 'skill picker' pop-up. Each selected
skill displays its cells (scriptItems from scripts[i].scriptIndex to
scripts[i+1].scriptIndex - 1) as a row of 16x16 icons starting 16*12=192 px
into each row, left of which is the 32-byte script name.

State globals:
  g_skillPickerSelectedIdx (WORD)   = which script (row)
  g_skillCellGridPopupCellIdx (BYTE) = which cell within that script
  g_skillPickerOriginalValue         =

One-line label control shown next to each codeblock-operand slot in the

    byte 0   : flags (bit 0 = grid-popup mode; bit 10 = disabled)

    byte 5   : pressed flag

Sound-slot CRUD — RENAME entry at soundIdx.

Prompts TextInputDialog for a new name (up to 31 chars). Rejects duplicates unless the duplicate IS soundIdx itself (no-op). Copies up to sizeof(KgtSoundSlotEntry.name) into slot.soundHeaders[soundIdx].name, MarkSlotModified, ResetEditorViewState.

Sound-slot CRUD — INSERT new sound at index soundIdx.

Driven by the sounds panel; type 1=WAV (file dialog → .wav), 2=MIDI (file dialog → .mid), 3=CD-track (synthesised name 'CDtrack N').

Flow:
  - Abort if slot 255 is occupied (list full).
  - For types 1/2, ShowOpenFileDialog, strip path → name in lpString+0x80.
  - If overwriteExisting!=0: free the existing GlobalAlloc at slot[soundIdx] and reuse the slot.
  - Else: uniquify the proposed name (loops 'dddddddd%d' until unique), DialogBoxParam

Sound-slot CRUD — DELETE entry at soundIdx.

Flow:
  - Refuse to delete slot 0 (reserved silence/none).
  - Confirm with MessageBox ('Delete %s from game file?'), cancel on IDNO.
  - Walks all scripts: if any PLAY_SOUND (type 3) opcode references this soundIdx, prompts per-script ('%s skill will no longer use this sound'); any cancel aborts the delete.
  - Walks all scriptItems: for every PLAY_SOUND opcode, sound refs == soundIdx are set to 0 (silence), refs > soundIdx are decremented.
  - Same

Sound-slot CRUD — SWAP entries at idxA and idxB.

Swaps the two 42-byte KgtSoundSlotEntry records via a GlobalAlloc scratch buffer. Then walks all 65536 scriptItems; for each PLAY_SOUND opcode (type 3) calls SwapWordIndices(item, idxA, idxB) to remap the referenced sound index. Same update applied to the active stage or demo slot's script if the editor is in stage/demo mode.
Stops CD-audio playback: sends MCI_STOP (0x808) then MCI_CLOSE (0x804) to g_cdMciDeviceId and clears g_cdPlaybackActive. Previously mis-named PlayNextCDAudioTrack; renamed StopCDAudio.

Opens the CD-audio MCI device and plays track g_cdRequestedTrack. Probes drive letters if the first attempt fails. Uses command codes 0x803=MCI_OPEN, 0x80D=MCI_SET, 0x806=MCI_PLAY (with MCI_NOTIFY=0x0D flags), 0x804=MCI_CLOSE.
Initializes CD audio: stores track/loop params, seeds drive letter 'A' (0x41+0=0x41), calls PlayCDAudioTrack; on failure iterates drive letters probing IsDriveCDROM.
Stops MIDI playback (MCI_STOP + MCI_CLOSE on g_midiMciDeviceId) and clears g_midiPlaybackActive.
Deletes %WINDIR%\enterbrain2dfightermak.mid (legacy Fighter Maker-era temp file - the current MIDI uses enterbrain2dkgt2.mid).

Opens/starts playback of %WINDIR%\enterbrain2dkgt2.mid via MCI 'sequencer'. Despite the name, does not play WAV - only MIDI. Called by PlayMidiFromSoundEntry after it writes the MIDI bytes to that temp file.
Writes sound-entry's MIDI data to %WINDIR%\enterbrain2dkgt2.mid, stops any current playback, then calls StartMidiPlayback (formerly PlaySoundEffect) to play it.

InitializeMediaPlayback [MISNOMER]: actually RegisterKeyInputClass. Registers WNDCLASSA {lpfnWndProc=KeyInputCaptureWndProc, lpszClassName='KeyInput', hbrBackground=COLOR_WINDOW}. Should be renamed RegisterKeyInputCaptureClass.

KeyInputCaptureWndProc: modal keyboard-capture popup for rebinding shortcuts. WM_PAINT (0xF): draws 'Press a new key or Escape' prompt + current shortcut label. WM_KEYDOWN (0x100): reads key + modifiers (Ctrl/Shift/Alt state via GetAsyncKeyState) into g_keyboardShortcutTable[g_shortcutSlotToRebind], calls UpdateMenuState, DestroyWindow. ESC cancels. Triggered from HandleMenuCommand cases 0xCE4..0xD06 (menu IDs 3300-3334) via CreateKeyInputCaptureWindow. Class name 'KeyInput' registered separatel

CreateKeyInputCaptureWindow: spawns the 'KeyInput' class popup (0x200 x 64 px, no style) for shortcut-rebind. Called from HandleMenuCommand with g_shortcutSlotToRebind preset to the menu-item ID minus 3300.

Test-play launcher.

1. ValidateGameData(hWnd) — dirty/missing-resource checks.
2. LoadTestPlaySettings — read %app%.kgt2testplay.ini or similar test-play prefs.
3. DialogBoxParam(DIALOG_TESTGAMESETUP, TestGameSettingsDialogProc) — UI to pick output dir, team count, replay mode, etc.
4. ValidatePlayerSettings(hWnd) — ensure characters/stage/demo are valid.
5. CleanupPreviewState, SaveProjectSettings, SaveTestPlaySettings.
6. SaveProjectFile(currentPath, promptForName=0, isTestPlay=1) — exports .

Launches the test build: runs TestGameSettingsDialog, saves settings, saves project (isTestPlay=1), ChDir to install dir, then WinExec("KGT2nd_GAME.exe /T") or "/F" depending on g_testPlayLaunchMode (0=/T normal, 1=/F fight-replay). No command-line flag means run game normally.

ChooseColorForPalette: ChooseColorA wrapper used to edit the global UI-palette slots starting at g_ui_colors[colorSlotIdx] (15-bit RGB555 packed). Decodes RGB555 -> 8-bit-per-channel for the common dialog, then re-quantizes to 5-bit and re-packs after the user confirms.

ENGINE BUG: 'Objects visually delayed by 1 frame when creating/destroying' -- CreateAnimationObject allocates & zeroes a slot in unk_775900[1024] but does NOT run ExecuteAnimationScript on the new object this frame. The object's first [I] keepTime is only evaluated next tick. Similarly destroyed objects are cleared from array but their last-drawn sprite persists one frame. Logic runs at normal cadence but the render snapshot is 1 frame stale. See docs/editor/engine_bugs_mapped.md.

ENGINE BUG ROOT: 'C Cancel doesn't clear' -- if new anim id == current (state+48), this returns WITHOUT calling ClearObjectFlag297. Means a self-cancel leaves [C] cancel ptr at state+0x129 stale, re-triggering each frame. See docs/editor/engine_bugs_mapped.md.

ENGINE BUG: Write that causes 'attacks out of blockstun +1 startup' -- ProcessObjectPhysics sets state+0x3C=0 (wait counter) when consuming a queued cancel target from state+0x64. The dispatcher's wait-check at 0x43A0F8 runs right after physics and sees wait=0, enters the dispatch loop, but the script item index was just set to scriptStart, so the first [I] keepTime gets added on top of this frame. Effect: first frame of new move is held for 2 frames. See docs/editor/engine_bugs_mapped.md.

ENGINE BUG: 'Attacks immediately out of blockstun have +1 startup' -- this is one of the ChangeObjectAnimation sites called from ProcessObjectPhysics idle-fallback (0x439B31). The blockstun-exit path at 0x439A6D writes state+0x3C=0 AND state+0x2C=scriptStart before this is reached, and the dispatcher at 0x43A0F8 reads state+0x3C right after the physics call. Net effect: one frame is consumed by the transition (physics runs, dispatcher sees new anim but immediately returns because state+0x3C was
[DISPATCHER] Switch on scriptItem->type (first byte). Cases 0..41 via jpt_43B818. See docs/editor/opcode_dispatcher.md

Writes v21+0x18/+0x1C/+0x20/+0x24 (moveX, moveY, accelX, accelY). Sign inverted when facing-flipped (*(v21+92)&1). | jumptable 0043A17F case 1

[DS] Detect Skill (type=2, not in HK enum). esi[1] = subType 1..6 (Landing, Attack Hits, Defending, Hit the Wall, in offset, While throw do). esi[2..4] = jumpId:jumpPos packed. Sub-dispatch at jpt_43A4AA writes v21+0x64/0x68/0x6C/0x70/0x74/0x78 with the target. | jumptable 0043A17F case 2

[DS] subType 1 'Landing' writes v21+0x64 (per-entity). ENGINE BUG: '[DS] triggers from any object created by the entity' -- the hit-resolution code checks the PARENT player's DS slots when any of its child objects connect a hit, not just the object that actually hit. So a [DS] set on the player's active script fires when a hitspark (child object) connects, which is rarely the intended behavior. See docs/editor/engine_bugs_mapped.md. | jumptable 0043A4AA case 1

[DS] subType 6 'While throw do' writes v21+0x78 (throw slot). ENGINE BUG: '[DS] Throw cannot trigger from inside objects' -- the throw-input consumer reads the PLAYER's +0x78, not the object's. Since [DS] here writes to the CURRENT entity (child object if executed inside object), the player's throw slot stays empty. See docs/editor/engine_bugs_mapped.md. | jumptable 0043A4AA case 6

[PS] Player Stop (type=26). ENGINE BUG: 'Physics can still progress while Pausing' -- the object-pause loop at 0x43B0A5..0x43B0D1 only freezes objects that have flag 0x20000000 set at offset 0. This flag is set by the image renderer when an object has a valid sprite, so image-less objects (pure logic, or objects whose [I] hasn't run yet due to creation-delay) are never paused. Workarounds 'copy from Wonderful World' / 'no image used' both circumvent this filter. See docs/editor/engine_bugs_mappe

jumptable 0043A4AA default case
[INIT PATH] First-frame initialization. *(dword_7D5190+338)==0 on entry. Sets init-done flag, initializes state slots, sets default hit-reaction config based on object type (v21+342: 1/2/3/4/5/6/7).
kgt2nd class index 51 (g_windowClassBuffers[51]) = 0x7D6EB0. Slot list (character/stage/demo sidebar).

jumptable 0043B9AE default case, cases 2-4,6
jumptable 0043BEA1 default case, cases 278-511

Bundles current project + all loaded characters/stages/demos into the test-play directory (g_testPlayOutputDir@0x451B20). For each slot: copies KGT2nd_GAME.exe, writes <name>.kgt / <name>.player / <name>.stage / <name>.demo with possibly-encrypted 2DKGT2G signature (vs plain 2DKGT2K). Called by the TestPlayExportDlgProc@0x43CB80 dialog.
Test-play Export progress dialog. First OK click calls ExportTestPlayData; updates status labels; re-enables OK button. Second OK click closes with result=1.

Verifies the test-play output dir is writable: copies %WINDIR%\\win.ini to <outDir>\\write-test-delete.txt via SHFileOperationA. Returns 1 on failure (abort export), 0 on success (and deletes the test file).
